channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Palestinian embassy in Egypt says Rafah crossing will reopen Monday for people returning to Gaza
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — The Rafah border crossing between Gaza and Egypt will reopen Monday for people returning to Gaza, the Palestinian embassy in Egypt said Saturday, but the territory's sole gateway to the outside world will remain closed to people trying to leave.
item.dc:creator: TOQA EZZIDIN, SAM MEDNICK and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Heavy security after deadly violence in Kenya, as thousands mourn former Prime Minister Raila Odinga
item.description: NAIROBI, Kenya (AP) — Heavy security was deployed to Kenya’s western city of Kisumu before the final public viewing of the body of former Prime Minister Raila Odinga on Saturday, after five people died in the last two public funeral events.
item.dc:creator: EVELYNE MUSAMBI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Work begins to restore power to Ukraine’s Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant
item.description: Work has begun to repair the damaged power supply to Ukraine’s Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant, the head of the U.N.’s nuclear watchdog said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Afghan and Pakistani delegations in Doha for crisis talks over cross-border violence
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — Afghan and Pakistani delegations were in the Qatari capital, Doha, on Saturday, for talks on defusing the deadliest crisis between them in several years, after more than a week of fighting killed dozens of people and injured hundreds on both sides.
item.dc:creator: RIAZAT BUTT, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Zelensky says Trump didn’t say ‘yes’ to Tomahawk missiles request
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky on Friday said President Trump poured cold water on his request to provide the Eastern European nation with Tomahawk missiles amid its over three-years-long war with Russia. “It's very difficult just to operate only with Ukrainian drones.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Israel identifies remains of 10th deceased hostage handed over by Hamas
item.description: Israel received another hostage's remains from Hamas via the Red Cross, identifying the deceased as Eliyahu Margalit, who was killed on Oct. 7, 2023.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: One scandal too many forces UK monarchy to sideline Prince Andrew after years of tabloid fodder
item.description: Prince Andrew has been banished from the royal whirl after emails emerged this week showing that he had remained in contact with the convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein longer than he previously admitted.
item.dc:creator: Danica Kirka, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A look at Prince Andrew's antics and scandals that have tried royal patience for decades
item.description: Britain’s Prince Andrew has been forced to relinquish use of his remaining royal titles after the latest revelations about his relationship with the convicted pedophile Jeffrey Epstein proved one scandal too many for his brother, King Charles III.
item.dc:creator: Danica Kirka, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Deadly floods in Mexico another sign of need for improved severe weather warnings
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — The most recent torrential rains in east-central Mexico, which have left at least 72 dead and dozens more missing, have raised questions again about the government’s ability to alert people to severe weather in time.
item.dc:creator: MARÍA VERZA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Daily struggles persist in Gaza even as ceasefire offers some respite
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Bassil Naggar can finally sleep without getting jolted awake by the sounds of Israeli airstrikes.
item.dc:creator: TOQA EZZIDIN, WAFAA SHURAFA and MARIAM FAM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: How Trump's approval shifted after the ceasefire in Gaza, according to a new AP-NORC poll
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — More U.S. adults support President Donald Trump's handling of the conflict in Israel after he brokered a ceasefire deal in Gaza, a new AP-NORC poll finds — but his approval ratings on domestic issues remain weak.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, The Hill

item.title: Watch live: Trump meets with Ukraine's Zelensky at White House
item.description: President Trump will host Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky at the White House on Friday for the third time since returning to office in January. The first visit was a train wreck; the second, which involved European leaders, went more smoothly.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, The Hill

item.title: Iran’s execution rate tops 1,000 this year as death row inmates launch hunger strike
item.description: Iranian death row prisoners call on U.N. Secretary General António Guterres to intervene as the regime executes over 1,000 people since the start of the year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: King to pray with pope in first since 1534
item.description: VATICAN CITY/LONDON, Oct 17 (Reuters) - Britain's King Charles and Queen Camilla will meet Pope Leo at the Vatican next week in a visit seen as a sign of closening ties between the Catholic Church and the Anglican Communion, five centuries after their turbulent separation.
item.dc:creator: Joshua McElwee and Muvija M, NewsNation

item.title: Russia convicts 15 captured Ukrainian soldiers on terrorism charges
item.description: A court in southern Russia convicted 15 captured Ukrainian soldiers on terrorism charges Friday after a trial that Kyiv denounced as a sham and a violation of international law.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mongolia's prime minister ousted after 4 months in ruling party feud
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — Mongolia ’s parliament has voted to oust the prime minister in an unusually public power struggle within the ruling Mongolian People’s Party.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: King Charles III's visit to Vatican marks a historic step in path of unity between two churches
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — The Catholic Church and Church of England, divided for centuries over issues that now include the ordination of female priests, will take a historic step on the path to unity next week when Britain’s King Charles III and Pope Leo XIV pray together in the Sistine Chapel, officials
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russian barrage causes blackouts in Ukraine as Zelenskyy seeks Trump's help
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia battered Ukraine’s energy facilities with hundreds of drones and dozens of missiles in its latest heavy bombardment of the country’s power grid, authorities said Thursday, as Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy prepared to ask President Donald Trump at a White House m
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Hamas captors once held ordinary jobs
item.description: Israeli hostage Tal Shoham describes increased faith and gratitude after enduring torture and starvation during 500 days held by Hamas in Gaza tunnels.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump: 'No choice but to go in and kill' Hamas if Gaza bloodshed doesn't stop
item.description: President Trump said Thursday that if Hamas does not heed repeated warnings to stop bloodshed in Gaza, the U.S. will have “no choice” but to use lethal force. “If Hamas continues to kill people in Gaza, which was not the Deal, we will have no choice but to go in and kill them.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: The Latest: Israel identifies remains of 2 more hostages
item.description: Israel’s National Institute of Forensic Medicine on Thursday identified the remains of two more hostages returned from Gaza, as officials and families warned Hamas to hand over the bodies of those still held.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Former security guard at US Embassy overseas is convicted of spying for Russia and Iran
item.description: A former U.S. Embassy security guard in Norway was reportedly sentenced to three years and seven months after allegedly spying for Russia and Iran.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: IDF holds memorial ceremony at base attacked by Hamas on Oct. 7 honoring fallen troops
item.description: The Israel Defense Forces (IDF) remembered the second anniversary of the Oct. 7, 2023, attacks on Nahal Oz with a ceremony at the base this week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia releases JFK files to Rep. Anna Paulina Luna
item.description: The transfer of the 350-page document was confirmed this week by Russia’s ambassador to the U.S. and Luna, who chairs a House panel on government secrets.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Ukrainian officials meet with US weapons manufacturers before Trump-Zelenskyy talks
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A Ukrainian government delegation has met with prominent American weapons manufacturers during a U.S. visit, a senior Kyiv official said Wednesday, before President Volodymyr Zelenskyy’s meeting with U.S. counterpart Donald Trump at the White House later this week.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Pakistan and Taliban agree to 48-hour ceasefire after renewed fighting kills dozens
item.description: A temporary ceasefire has been agreed to after border violence escalated between Pakistan and Afghanistan over the weekend as both sides blame each other for attacks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Remains released by Hamas not a hostage, Israel's military says
item.description: The original framework of a U.S.-brokered ceasefire required Hamas to return all 28 deceased hostages Monday.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Trump’s swipe at ‘weak’ leaders highlights the shifting global influence of Western nations
item.description: Amid Trump’s visit to Egypt and Israel to celebrate the release of hostages held by Hamas and to usher in a new era of Mideast peace, some say Western influence is waning.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: IDF says body turned over by Hamas doesn't match any hostages
item.description: The Israel Defense Forces announced that a corpse surrendered by Hamas was not a match for any of the hostages who are supposed to be released.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia using new, more precise drone tech to attack Ukraine’s vital rail network
item.description: Since the start of Russia's full-scale invasion in early 2022, railway officials have publicly reported about roughly one attack on railways per week.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: No end in sight to government shutdown as House speaker refuses to negotiate
item.description: The shutdown entered its 14th day with the Senate returning from its holiday break, Republican House Speaker Mike Johnson refusing to call lawmakers back to Washington and President Donald Trump’s budget director vowing to keep firing federal workers.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Hamas holds public executions, tightens grip on Gaza
item.description: CAIRO/JERUSALEM, Oct 14 (Reuters) - Israel restricted aid into Gaza and kept the enclave's border shut on Tuesday while re-emergent Hamas fighters demonstrated their grip by executing men in the street, darkening the outlook for U.S. President Donald Trump's plan to end the war.
item.dc:creator: Nidal al-Mughrabi and Maayan Lubell, NewsNation

item.title: Therapist explains what Hamas hostages are feeling
item.description: Dr. Rebecca Bailey joins "Banfield" to discuss the mental process the former Hamas hostages will be going through.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's power worldwide has 'increased 1,000%': Bill O'Reilly
item.description: Sunday marked 737 days since the hostages were taken, and all 20 hostages remaining alive were released Monday.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: How the US brokered the Gaza ceasefire deal
item.description: The ceasefire between Israel and Hamas saw the release of Israeli hostages and Palestinian prisoners and detainees.
item.dc:creator: Joe Khalil, NewsNation

item.title: Photos: Israeli, Palestinian families celebrate return of hostages, prisoners
item.description: The last remaining living Israeli hostages were released as well as more than 1,900 detained Palestinians.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: Trump to Knesset: Israel has won ‘all that they can’ in military campaign
item.description: President Trump on Monday urged Israeli leaders to turn the page from warfare to peace, telling the Knesset that Israel had won “all that they can by force of arms” and expressing optimism about the region’s future.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9
